# Investigative Narrative — Case SRL18-FILE-DISK

*Generated by Council-SIFT from its verification substrate (`claw-memory-core`). Every claim below is
**COUNCIL_VERIFIED**: it survived adversarial review by the forensic seats, the evidence it cites resolves
in real tool output, and it is traceable to the exact tool execution; concrete-command receipts may be rerun with `csift trace --rerun`, while stored-output rehash remains the baseline integrity check.*

5 of 6 drafted finding(s) are Council-verified; 1 draft(s) were

## Findings

### F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-001 — Recursive Sleuth Kit fls of the NTFS volume (offset 0), filtere… *(confidence: HIGH)*

Recursive Sleuth Kit fls of the NTFS volume (offset 0), filtered to WinRAR file names, returned four entries, each carrying the '*' deletion marker: Rar.exe (MFT 124369-128-3), UnRAR.exe (MFT 124371-128-3), WinRAR.exe (MFT 124372-128-3), and WinRAR.chm (MFT 124377-128-3). The leading '-/r' type and '*' indicate deleted, unallocated files.. A WinRAR installation -- including the Rar.exe command-line archiver, which intruders routinely use to compress (stage) collected data prior to exfiltration -- was present on this file server and has been deleted (its parent directory C:\Program Files (x86)\WinRAR is also deleted, so these now appear as orphaned files). This corroborates, at the file-presence level, the memory-analysis flag that Rar.exe was used for staging on this host. The presence and deletion of the tool does NOT by itself prove the tool was executed; no Prefetch entry and no matching Amcache record were found to confirm execution..

- **Evidence pointer:** `base-file-cdrive.E01 -- NTFS volume (offset 0), C:\Program Files (x86)\WinRAR (deleted)#MFT 124369 (Rar.exe), 124371 (UnRAR.exe), 124372 (WinRAR.exe), 124377 (WinRAR.chm)` — tool `fls (The Sleuth Kit)`, command `fls -r -o 0 "/mnt/evidence/Compromised APT Attack Scenarios/SRL 2018/base-file-cdrive.E01…`
- **Cited evidence:** `-/r * 124369-128-3:`, `Rar.exe`, `UnRAR.exe`, `WinRAR.exe`, `WinRAR.chm` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 c908a5fd82c37778…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-001`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-001` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `a78dc236fccad682…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-003 — istat of MFT entry 124753 reports 'Not Allocated File' with a n… *(confidence: MEDIUM)*

istat of MFT entry 124753 reports 'Not Allocated File' with a non-resident $DATA stream of Actual Size 203196174 bytes (~194 MB). Recorded names include the 8.3 name 'WINDOW~1.ZZZ' and a long name composed of repeated 'Z' characters ending in a .ZZZ extension. Created 2018-09-05 13:21:53 (UTC), File Modified 2018-09-06 16:22:14 (UTC). icat of the first 16 data bytes returns all zeros.. Inode 124753 is a deleted ~194 MB file. Reading the start of its $DATA stream with icat returns zero bytes, so the content is not recoverable from these clusters. Its recorded name has been changed to a non-descriptive string of repeated 'Z' characters with a .ZZZ extension. fls reconstructs this same inode's path from the parent directory index as '...\research\Rare-Earth Elements\M&A Targets.zip', placing it as a sibling of the staged M&A research directory (see related finding). So a ~194 MB file associated by path with that M&A research directory was created 2018-09-05 and deleted with its content overwritten by 2018-09-06. The file's actual contents cannot be confirmed because the data is zeroed; this finding establishes only the existence, size, timing, deletion, name change, and content-overwrite of the file -- not the identity of any tool or actor responsible, nor that data left the host.. This is the **corrected** finding — an initial, stronger reading was refuted by the Council (see *Self-correction*, below) and the analyst re-filed only what the evidence supports.

- **Evidence pointer:** `base-file-cdrive.E01 -- NTFS volume (offset 0), deleted file inode 124753 (fls path: Windows/Logs/WindowsServerBackup/7.15/research/Rare-Earth Elements/M&A Targets.zip)#MFT 124753; $DATA actual size 203196174; Created 2018-09-05 13:21:53 UTC` — tool `istat + icat (The Sleuth Kit)`, command `istat -o 0 IMG 124753 | grep -E 'Not Allocated File|Actual Size|Created:|File Modified:|N…`
- **Cited evidence:** `Not Allocated File`, `Actual Size: 203196174`, `2018-09-05 13:21:53.240167900 (UTC)`, `2018-09-06 16:22:14.756551100 (UTC)`, `WINDOW~1.ZZZ`, `0000 0000 0000 0000` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 30cfb60736cc19bf…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-003`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-003` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `0931122c6e01fdf8…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-004 — Recursive fls (full paths, offset 0) shows a DELETED directory … *(confidence: MEDIUM)*

Recursive fls (full paths, offset 0) shows a DELETED directory subtree under C:\Windows\Logs\WindowsServerBackup\7.15\research\Rare-Earth Elements\ named 'M&A Targets' (inode 124754-144-1), containing subdirectories 'Orocobre' (124755) and 'Tronox' (124779) with PDF documents (e.g., TRONOX_2015.pdf, inode 124789). A deleted sibling file 'M&A Targets.zip' (inode 124753-128-1) sits in the same 'Rare-Earth Elements' directory. PDFs in the tree carry ':Zone.Identifier' alternate data streams (e.g., on 16Jan18_OREInvestorPresentation.pdf).. A collection of external research on rare-earth-element companies was assembled on this file server and then deleted. The directory name 'M&A Targets' and its contents (annual reports, investor presentations, technical reports for the named companies) indicate gathered merger-and-acquisition research. The ':Zone.Identifier' alternate data streams indicate the PDFs were downloaded from another security zone (typically the internet). The subtree sits under a path that mimics the Windows Server Backup directory rather than a normal user document area, and the deleted 'M&A Targets.zip' (related finding on inode 124753) is co-located with it. This is consistent with aggregation of sensitive business research into one tree that was then compressed and removed. It does not by itself establish who created it, nor that the data was transferred off the host.. This is the **corrected** finding — an initial, stronger reading was refuted by the Council (see *Self-correction*, below) and the analyst re-filed only what the evidence supports.

- **Evidence pointer:** `base-file-cdrive.E01 -- NTFS volume (offset 0), deleted subtree under Windows/Logs/WindowsServerBackup/7.15/research/Rare-Earth Elements/M&A Targets#MFT 124754 (M&A Targets dir), 124755 (Orocobre), 124779 (Tronox), 124789 (TRONOX_2015.pdf), 124753 (M&A Targets.zip)` — tool `fls (The Sleuth Kit)`, command `fls -r -p -o 0 IMG | grep -E 'research/Rare-Earth Elements/M&A Targets'`
- **Cited evidence:** `124754-144-1:`, `Windows/Logs/WindowsServerBackup/7.15/research/Rare-Earth Elements/M&A Targets`, `Windows/Logs/WindowsServerBackup/7.15/research/Rare-Earth Elements/M&A Targets/Orocobre`, `Windows/Logs/WindowsServerBackup/7.15/research/Rare-Earth Elements/M&A Targets/Tronox`, `Windows/Logs/WindowsServerBackup/7.15/research/Rare-Earth Elements/M&A Targets.zip`, `Windows/Logs/WindowsServerBackup/7.15/research/Rare-Earth Elements/M&A Targets/Orocobre/2018 Results/16Jan18_OREInvestorPresentation.pdf:Zone.Identifier` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 7f0fcde5d8100826…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-004`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-004` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `44947807e9f00333…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-005 — istat of MFT entry 124824 reports 'Not Allocated File' with a $… *(confidence: MEDIUM)*

istat of MFT entry 124824 reports 'Not Allocated File' with a $DATA Actual Size of 862864067 bytes (~823 MB). Two recorded names are the 8.3 name 'WINDOZ~1.ZZZ' and the long name 'windoZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZ.ZZZ'; both have Parent MFT Entry: 5. Created 2018-09-05 14:32:36 (UTC), File Modified 2018-09-06 16:33:25 (UTC). icat returned 0 bytes of recoverable data (recovered_bytes=0).. Inode 124824 is a deleted ~823 MB file located in the NTFS root directory (Parent MFT Entry 5 is the volume root), i.e. at C:\. Its name has been changed to a non-descriptive run of repeated 'Z' characters with a .ZZZ extension, and icat recovers no data, so its content is overwritten and unrecoverable. Its creation (2018-09-05 14:32) and modification (2018-09-06 16:33) fall in the same window as the deleted WinRAR tooling (finding F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-001) and the ~194 MB deleted file (F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-003). This is a second large file with an obfuscated name and overwritten content. The finding establishes the file's existence, size, root location, timing, name change, and content-overwrite only -- not the responsible tool or actor, the original contents, nor any transfer off the host.. This is the **corrected** finding — an initial, stronger reading was refuted by the Council (see *Self-correction*, below) and the analyst re-filed only what the evidence supports.

- **Evidence pointer:** `base-file-cdrive.E01 -- NTFS volume (offset 0), deleted file inode 124824 at volume root (C:\)#MFT 124824; Parent MFT Entry 5 (root); $DATA actual size 862864067; Created 2018-09-05 14:32:36 UTC` — tool `istat + icat (The Sleuth Kit)`, command `istat -o 0 IMG 124824 | grep -E 'Not Allocated File|Actual Size|Created:|File Modified:|N…`
- **Cited evidence:** `Not Allocated File`, `Actual Size: 862864067`, `2018-09-05 14:32:36.703124300 (UTC)`, `2018-09-06 16:33:25.296374000 (UTC)`, `WINDOZ~1.ZZZ`, `windoZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZ.ZZZ`, `Parent MFT Entry: 5`, `recovered_bytes=0` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 ab7ce7e02dd22ce5…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-005`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-005` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `ae68bf235cbe60b9…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-006 — fls shows two files named StarFury.zip: an allocated (live) fil… *(confidence: MEDIUM)*

fls shows two files named StarFury.zip: an allocated (live) file at Shares/shieldbase-share/R&D/StarFury.zip (inode 120557-128-1) and a deleted file at Windows/Logs/WindowsServerBackup/7.13/R&D/StarFury.zip (inode 124702-128-1). istat of the live copy reports 'Allocated File', Created 2018-07-11 16:16:06 (UTC), File Modified 2012-03-12 20:49:00 (UTC), Actual Size 724919. istat of the deleted copy reports 'Not Allocated File', Created 2018-09-05 13:24:07 (UTC), File Modified 2018-09-06 16:22:02 (UTC), Actual Size 724919.. A file with the same name and identical byte size (724919) as the legitimate R&D-share file StarFury.zip was present, deleted, in the WindowsServerBackup\7.13\R&D staging subtree, created 2018-09-05 and deleted 2018-09-06 -- the same window as the M&A research tree and WinRAR tooling. The matching size indicates the staging-tree file is a copy of the genuine R&D share content, showing that the WindowsServerBackup subtree held copies of real file-share data, not just downloaded research. The live R&D-share StarFury.zip (created 2018-07-11, content dated 2012) is still allocated and its timestamps predate the September 2018 activity, so the original share file is intact and is NOT itself an attacker artifact -- only the deleted staging copy is. Identical size is strong but not cryptographic proof of identical content, and this finding does not establish off-host transfer.. This is the **corrected** finding — an initial, stronger reading was refuted by the Council (see *Self-correction*, below) and the analyst re-filed only what the evidence supports.

- **Evidence pointer:** `base-file-cdrive.E01 -- NTFS volume (offset 0); live inode 120557 (Shares/shieldbase-share/R&D/StarFury.zip) vs deleted inode 124702 (Windows/Logs/WindowsServerBackup/7.13/R&D/StarFury.zip)#MFT 120557 (live, allocated) and MFT 124702 (deleted); both Actual Size 724919` — tool `fls + istat (The Sleuth Kit)`, command `fls -r -p -o 0 IMG | grep StarFury.zip ; istat -o 0 IMG 120557 ; istat -o 0 IMG 124702`
- **Cited evidence:** `Shares/shieldbase-share/R&D/StarFury.zip`, `Windows/Logs/WindowsServerBackup/7.13/R&D/StarFury.zip`, `Not Allocated File`, `Actual Size: 724919`, `2018-07-11 16:16:06.501542000 (UTC)`, `2012-03-12 20:49:00.000000000 (UTC)`, `2018-09-05 13:24:07.257129200 (UTC)`, `2018-09-06 16:22:02.115333800 (UTC)` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 7ae2c713331f93a3…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-006`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-006` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `9c48d16b65aefd25…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

## What the evidence does NOT support (Council-enforced discipline)

The Council refused the following stronger readings because the available artifacts do not establish them.
They are recorded here explicitly so the report distinguishes **confirmed** from **inferred or unfounded**:

- Cited token(s) NOT present in the tool output as a standalone token (hallucinated/unsupported): windowsserverbackupZZ.ZZZ.
- Inference overreach (ATTRIBUTION): attribution to a specific actor cannot be established from one artifact — corroborate with intel + multiple artifacts. Offending clause: "renaming a file to a single-repeated-character name and zeroing its contents is the characteristic signature of a secure-deletion / file-wip".

## Self-correction record (no human intervention)

- **F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-002** was drafted as: *"istat of MFT entry 124753 reports 'Not Allocated File' with a non-resident $DAT… → Inode 124753 is a deleted ~194 MB file whose data clusters read back as zeros, so its con…"*, then **bounced** by the Council:
  > [seat:citation] Cited token(s) NOT present in the tool output as a standalone token (hallucinated/unsupported): windowsserverbackupZZ.ZZZ. [seat:inference] Inference overreach (ATTRIBUTION): attribution to a specific actor cannot be establ…

  The analyst read the objection and self-corrected — re-filing the supported claim now shown under *Findings*.

## Confidence & limitations

- Confidence is stated per finding (HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / SPECULATIVE) and reflects what a single artifact
  can bear; nothing here is asserted beyond the cited evidence.
- "Observation" is the literal tool output; "interpretation" is the analyst's inference. The Council's
  Citation, Tool-semantics, Contradiction, Inference, and Scope seats independently checked the leap between them; Synthesis aggregated the verdicts.
- Every finding traces to its tool execution (`csift trace F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-001`); concrete-command receipts can additionally be re-executed against evidence with `csift trace --rerun …`. Council Receipts are hash-chained.
- This narrative is generated from the audit substrate, not hand-written — it cannot contain a claim that
  is not a verified node in the graph.
